---
name: Rémi
surname: Thériault
# profilepic: "img/profile.png"
position: "Postdoctoral Fellow & Incoming Assistant Professor"
qualifications: "PhD"
address: "New York University, New York, USA"
phone: (332) 400-6781
www: remi-theriault.com
email: "remi.theriault@nyu.edu"
linkedin: rempsyc
orcid: "0000-0003-4315-6788"
# googlescholar: NrfwEncAAAAJ
github: rempsyc
# twitter: RemiTPsyc
# pronouns: "il/lui"

aboutme: |
  \RaggedRight My training and interests lie at the intersection of social psychology, neuroscience, and positive psychology. Over the past decade, my research has focused on developing and testing innovative psychological interventions to positively transform our relationships to others and the world. In my postdoctoral research, I attempt to reduce affective polarization by appealing to shared social identities. I am also passionate about open science, reproducible science, metascience, and data science using R (this CV was [made in R](https://github.com/rempsyc/cv)).

date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%B %Y')`"
headcolor: "333333" # my shirt on the profil pic is 839AC4, but we take a bit lighter for better look (6FA3CE)
# Ok recent feedback was to not use colour at all here, so instead we go with the dark blue of my suit
# (21242D) so it doesn't even look that obvious but we still have a little something.
# #333333 is the default grey.
urlcolor: blue
linkcolor: blue
output: 
  vitae::awesomecv:
    page_total: true
    font_scale: 1.0
header-includes:
  - \usepackage[gen]{eurosym}
  - \input{derived/cv-settings.tex}
papersize: letterpaper
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

###################################
#### YOUR SETTINGS (MUST EDIT) ####
###################################

# Language: "EN" for English, "FR" for French
language <- "EN"

# Whether to print a shortened version of the CV
short <- FALSE

# How your name appears in publications (used for auto-bolding)
author_bold <- "Thériault, R."

# Your Google Scholar profile ID (from your profile URL)
scholar.profile <- "NrfwEncAAAAJ"

# Your Google Scholar short author name
author.name <- "rém thériault"
# Note: correct name may vary (e.g., if you inconsistently used middle name initials)
# Dominique Makowski's was simply "d makowski", so the pattern is unclear
# But usually it seems 1-3 letters from first name plus full last name
# When in doubt, the correct author name can be obtained through:
# get_scholar_name(scholar.profile) # (after defining 'scholar.profile' above)

###############################
#### OPTIONAL SETTINGS ########
###############################

# Cache settings: avoid hitting Google Scholar on every knit
refresh_scholar <- FALSE  # Set to TRUE to force a fresh fetch
cache_days <- 1           # Re-fetch automatically after this many days

# Publications to exclude from Google Scholar (e.g., preprints or R packages already listed elsewhere)
# Use format: "LastName (Year)" — add letter suffix for disambiguation: "LastName (Yearb)"
scholar_exclude <- c(
  "Lukmanova (NA)", 
  "Lüdecke (2019)",
  "Makowski (2020)",
  "Ben‑Shachar (2022)",
  "Lüdecke (2022)",
  "Makowski (2023)", 
  "Lüdecke (2023)",
  "Brodeur (2024)",
  "Thériault (2024b)",
  "Thériault (2025)"
)

# Show a preview table of all Google Scholar entries with exclusion keys.
# This helps identify what to add/remove in `scholar_exclude`.
show_scholar_exclusion_preview <- FALSE

# Maximum number of rows to show in the exclusion preview table.
# Use Inf to display all entries.
scholar_exclusion_preview_n <- Inf

# Fix author name inconsistencies from Google Scholar (named vector: wrong = correct)
scholar_name_fixes <- c("R Thériault" = "Rém Thériault")

# Fix author ORDER for specific publications (named list: wrong order = correct order)
# Useful for co-first-author papers where you want to be listed first so it counts
# toward your number of first-author publications in the impact metrics.
# Use pubids from your Google Scholar profile URL (click a paper → see ?pubid= in URL)
scholar_author_corrections <- list(
  # Swap co-first authors so this paper counts as first-author:
  "M Miglianico, Rém Thériault" = "Rém Thériault, M Miglianico"
)

# Append missing author to specific publications (vector of pubids)
scholar_append_author <- c("Zph67rFs4hoC", "IWHjjKOFINEC")

# Header color for the CV
headcolor <- "0a0a0a"

# Secondary text color for dates and other muted right-column text.
# Use "000000" for full black or "333333" for a softer near-black.
secondary_text_color <- "333333"

# Keep numbering for publications by default. Toggle to TRUE when you want
# numbering in talks, posters, awards, grants, software, etc. for easy counting.
count_everything <- FALSE

# Local size knobs that avoid changing the overall document font scale.
header_scale <- 1.1
baseline_text_size <- 10
entry_text_size <- 11
table_text_size <- 10
entry_row_stretch <- 1.2
table_row_stretch <- 0.6
footer_text_size <- 10
table_left_indent <- "0.3cm"

########################################
#### PACKAGE SETUP (DO NOT MODIFY) #####
########################################

# Loads and auto-installs if needed:
#   vitae, tidyverse, scholar, patchwork, see, rsvg, kableExtra, here
source("files/packages.R")

# To format links throughout the CV
source("files/functions.R")

dir.create("derived", showWarnings = FALSE)
writeLines(
  c(
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\ACVheaderScale}{%s}", header_scale),
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\acvEntryRowStretch}{%s}", entry_row_stretch),
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\acvEntryTitleTextSize}{%s}", latex_scaled_text_size(entry_text_size, 1.0)),
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\acvEntryMetaTextSize}{%s}", latex_scaled_text_size(entry_text_size, 0.8)),
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\acvEntryBodyTextSize}{%s}", latex_scaled_text_size(entry_text_size, 0.9)),
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\acvFooterTextSize}{%s}", latex_text_size(footer_text_size)),
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\acvHonorsTextSize}{%s}", latex_text_size(table_text_size)),
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\acvHonorsAmountSize}{%s}", latex_text_size(table_text_size)),
    sprintf("\\renewcommand{\\acvHonorsRowStretch}{%s}", table_row_stretch)
  ),
  "derived/cv-settings.tex"
)

########################
##### INSTRUCTIONS #####
########################

# Change the order of sections below as desired.
# Edit the individual files ("childs") to edit the content as desired.
# Add `eval = !short` to code chunks to make them optional for the short version

# See all instructions and tips here: https://github.com/rempsyc/cv

```

```{r Template color overrides, results = "asis"}
secondary_text_color_clean <- gsub("^#", "", secondary_text_color)
cat(sprintf("\\definecolor{cvsecondarytext}{HTML}{%s}\n", secondary_text_color_clean))
cat("\\colorlet{graytext}{cvsecondarytext}\n")
cat("\\colorlet{lighttext}{cvsecondarytext}\n")
```

```{r Template baseline sizing, results = "asis"}
cat(latex_text_size(baseline_text_size), "\n")
```

```{r Template table sizing, results = "asis"}
```

```{r Appointments parent, child=c('sections/appointments.Rmd')}
```

```{r Education parent, child=c('sections/education.Rmd')}
```

```{r Grants parent, child=c('sections/grants.Rmd'), eval=T}
```

\pagebreak

```{r Awards parent, child=c('sections/awards.Rmd')}
```

\setlength{\parskip}{8pt}

```{r Teaching parent, child=c('sections/teaching.Rmd')}
```

```{r Publications parent, child=c('sections/publications.Rmd')}
```

```{r Preprints parent, child=c('sections/preprints.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Invited talks parent, child=c('sections/invited_talks.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Conference talks parent, child=c('sections/conference_talks.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Conference posters parent, child=c('sections/conference_posters.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Academic service parent, child=c('sections/service.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Software parent, child=c('sections/software.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Blogs parent, child=c('sections/blogs.Rmd'), eval = F}
```

```{r Media coverage parent, child=c('sections/media_coverage.Rmd'), eval = F}
```

```{r References parent, child=c('sections/references.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

<!-- We skip the sections below for now. They are on the website and it was recommended to avoid them for the CV for applying to prof positions -->

```{r Other experience parent, child=c('sections/other_experience.Rmd'), eval = F}
```

```{r Leadership parent, child=c('sections/leadership.Rmd'), eval = F}
```

```{r Varia parent, child=c('sections/varia.Rmd'), eval = F}
```

```{r credit parent, child=c('sections/credit.Rmd'), eval = F}
```
